title: pouteria maclayana
intro: Pouteria maclayana, also known as the Mahogany or the Dabai tree, is a tropical evergreen plant native to the coastal regions of Malaysia and the Philippines. The plant is valued for its edible fruit and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The fruit, leaves, and bark of the plant have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, diarrhea, and skin infections, and have been shown to have health benefits such as reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, and is also used in traditional remedies for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties.
